title: leea coccinea
intro: Leea coccinea, also known as Leea indica or Leea rubra, is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a climbing shrub native to tropical Asia, commonly found in the forests and woodlands of India and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, and has been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Leea coccinea can be prepared as a herbal decoction, infusion, or tincture, and has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
